title: design_index
description: Schema for a design-bundle `_index.yaml` (kind: design_index). WHAT IT IS: the aggregate root of a design bundle — the member list, the inter-node design graph, status, and scope guard. WHAT IT DOES: pins reading order and the design's own edges so the bundle is itself a validatable graph (dogfood). WHAT IT DOES NOT DO: it does not validate the member .md nodes (each has its own schema), does not check cross-node closure (that is the projection engine), and does not carry node-level manifest data.

status.description: Lifecycle state of the design itself.

governance.description: Governance standing. pre-governance-input = not yet entered a governed run; governed = under an active run; ratified = council-accepted into canon.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the bundle was last revised. No fabricated per-edit times.

origin.description: Free-text provenance of the bundle (e.g. the session that produced it).

schema_dir.description: Relative dir holding the JSON-Schemas this bundle's YAML validates against.

members.description: Ordered list of member node files (reading order). Filenames relative to the bundle root.

edges.description: The design bundle as a graph: typed, provenance-tagged edges between member nodes (dogfoods the edge schema the design specifies).

src.description: Source node id (member basename without extension).

rel.description: Relation type between design nodes (closed vocabulary).

provenance.description: How the edge is known. For a design bundle these are asserted (a human-made design link) or derived (a structural dependency).

scope.description: v1 scope guard: what the design includes now vs. defers.
